IPC 302.Legal_Context: Punishment remains death or life imprisonment with fine. BNS 103(2) adds new offence of mob lynching.

IPC 307.Legal_Context: Punishment unchanged — up to 10 years; life imprisonment if hurt caused. Includes attempt by life convict.

IPC 378.Legal_Context: Definition retained; new sub-clause added for theft of electronic data.

IPC 379.Legal_Context: Same punishment; now includes specific provision for snatching.

IPC 415.Legal_Context: Definition of cheating retained from IPC with clearer language.

IPC 420.Description: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
IPC 420.Legal_Context: Consolidated with other fraud sections; punishment up to 7 years and fine.

IPC 124A.Description: Sedition (now: Acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India)
IPC 124A.Legal_Context: Significantly reworded. Old 'sedition' replaced with broader sovereignty offence. Punishment up to 7 years or life imprisonment.

IPC 375.Legal_Context: Expanded definition with additional aggravating factors specified.

IPC 376.Legal_Context: Minimum 10 years rigorous imprisonment; death penalty for repeat offenders or rape of minors under 12.

IPC 34.Description: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
IPC 34.Legal_Context: Moved to General Explanations chapter; principle unchanged.

IPC 120B.Legal_Context: Definition and punishment retained; now under chapter on abetment and conspiracy.

IPC 499.Legal_Context: Retained with same 10 exceptions. Punishment unchanged — up to 2 years, fine, or both.

CrPC 154.Legal_Context: FIR can now be filed electronically; zero FIR provision added for filing at any police station regardless of jurisdiction.
